keyPointsSummary: 'Going to' expresses a planned or intended future action
Formula: am/is/are + going to + base verb
Different from 'will' — 'going to' shows a plan already made

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं कल यह काम करने जा रहा हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I am going to do this work tomorrow.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह डॉक्टर बनने जा रही है।
quickQuiz.answer: She is going to become a doctor.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम अगले महीने घर बदलने जा रहे हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: We are going to change our house next month.

quickQuiz.hindi: वे शादी करने जा रहे हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: They are going to get married.

quickQuiz.hindi: क्या तुम आज बाहर जाने वाले हो?
quickQuiz.answer: Are you going to go out today?

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं यह किताब खरीदने जा रहा हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I am going to buy this book.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह अगले हफ्ते यात्रा करने जा रहा है।
quickQuiz.answer: He is going to travel next week.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम एक नया business शुरू करने जा रहे हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: We are going to start a new business.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं इस बारे में सोचने जा रहा हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I am going to think about this.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह यह project पूरा करने जा रही है।
quickQuiz.answer: She is going to finish this project.

mustRememberRules: 'Going to' expresses a planned or intended future action
Formula: am/is/are + going to + base verb
Different from 'will' — 'going to' shows a plan already made
